expectations.text: Response creates route files using createFileRoute
expectations.evidence: Both route files use createFileRoute: "export const Route = createFileRoute('/properties/')({...})" and "export const Route = createFileRoute('/properties/$propertyId')({...})".

expectations.text: Response includes a loader function that fetches data
expectations.evidence: Both routes define loader functions. The listing route: "loader: async () => { const response = await fetch('/api/properties'); ... }". The detail route: "loader: async ({ params }) => { ... await Promise.all([fetch(...), fetch(...)]) ... }".

expectations.text: Response uses notFound() for missing properties
expectations.evidence: Detail route imports notFound and uses it: "import { createFileRoute, notFound, Link } from '@tanstack/react-router';" and "if (propertyResponse.status === 404 || !propertyResponse.ok) { throw notFound(); }".

expectations.text: Response defines a pendingComponent with skeleton UI
expectations.evidence: Both routes define pendingComponent with skeleton UIs. For example: "pendingComponent: PropertyDetailSkeleton" with a full skeleton implementation using "animate-pulse" and "bg-muted rounded" placeholder elements.

expectations.text: Response uses Route.useLoaderData() to access data
expectations.evidence: Listing route: "const properties = Route.useLoaderData();". Detail route: "const { property, tenants } = Route.useLoaderData();".
